title: T039 send subscriber blocked: principal-less worker can't read/write RLS send tables
description:
The outbound send subscriber (internal/platform/notify/sender_subscriber.go) and the bounce path both call plain dbgen queries against RLS-protected tables (ticket_message, inbound_address) from the principal-less outbox worker (manyforge_app, NOBYPASSRLS, current_principal()=NULL).

Empirically (integration probe): authorized_businesses(NULL) returns 0 rows, so the ticket_message/inbound_address RLS USING predicate matches 0 rows. GetMessageDeliveryState/GetBusinessSystemInboundAddress return pgx.ErrNoRows and MarkMessageDelivered/MarkMessageFailed UPDATE 0 rows (silent no-op) when run via DB.WithTx.

Affected dbgen queries (all plain-table, all driven by the principal-less worker per their own doc comments): GetMessageDeliveryState, GetBusinessSystemInboundAddress, MarkMessageDelivered, MarkMessageFailed, GetOutboundMessageForBounce.

Fix (in-pattern, mirrors claim_outbox_batch / ingest_inbound_message in migrations 0014/0016): add a new migration (0019_*) with SECURITY DEFINER functions for the send/bounce delivery-state path, GRANT EXECUTE to manyforge_app, and repoint these db/query/ticketing.sql entries at them (RETURNS-TABLE DEFINERs via raw pgx per the plan's tech-stack note), then make generate. Out of scope for the T039 subscriber task (notify/main/config only); the plan's T035-prep landed these as plain-table queries and asserted 'no DEFINER change', which is the root oversight.

The subscriber + main.go wiring + config + DKIM are otherwise implemented and compile; only the DB layer below blocks the integration test (TestSendHappyPath/Suppressed/Idempotent).
close_reason: Fixed via migration 0019 SECURITY DEFINER (get_send_context + mark_message_delivery); subscriber repointed to raw-pgx DEFINER calls; send integration tests green.

title: US1 reply-token doesn't encode ticket's own id — p_hint_ticket threading fallback is dead
description:
In ingest_inbound_message (migrations/0014), the hint path resolves WHERE id = p_hint_ticket. But Go's inbox.Ingest mints reply_token over a FRESH uuid (not the ticket's row id, which the DB default generates), only to satisfy reply_token NOT NULL. So a later inbound reply carrying that token decodes to a uuid that never equals any ticket.id → the reply-token/VERP threading FALLBACK never matches. US1 threading is unaffected (it uses In-Reply-To/References header matching, which works + is tested). This breaks US2 outbound→inbound reply-token threading.

FIX (do in US2, TDD): write a failing test that (1) creates a ticket, (2) stamps Reply-To with SignReplyToken(ticket.id,key), (3) sends an inbound reply carrying that token with NO matching In-Reply-To header, (4) asserts it threads to the same ticket. It will fail. Then fix via the cleanest option: add p_ticket_id arg to ingest_inbound_message (new forward-only migration 0017, CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION) so Go generates the ticket uuid v7, signs the token over it, and passes both — making the stored ticket.reply_token coherent with the row id. (Alt: two-step UPDATE of reply_token after insert in the same tx.)

Minor related: attachment blob Key currently uses (tenantRoot, business, attachmentID, attachmentID) since ticket id isn't known pre-insert; functionally correct + tenant-scoped + unique, but a later task may want the real ticket id embedded (would require storing attachments after the ingest call). Flagged f2339ec.
notes:
RESOLVED. TDD surfaced TWO conspiring root causes, both fixed:
(1) id-coherence (documented): inbox.Ingest signed reply_token over uuid.New() (≠ ticket.id). Fix: migration 0017 adds p_ticket_id to ingest_inbound_message; Go now mints the ticket id (uuid v7), signs the token over it, and passes it as the new ticket's id.
(2) case-corruption (NEW, found via end-to-end RED): normalizeRecipient lowercased the WHOLE recipient incl. the case-sensitive base64url plus-token → VerifyReplyToken always failed. Fix: lowercase only the routing key (local+domain), preserve plus-token case verbatim.
Tests: internal/inbox/resolve_test.go (unit, case-preservation) + TestIngestRepliesThreadViaReplyToken (integration, end-to-end thread). Full gate green; migration 0017 up/down/up verified reversible.
close_reason: Reply-token/VERP threading fallback now works (two root causes fixed: id-coherence via migration 0017 p_ticket_id + plus-token case-preservation in normalizeRecipient). TDD red→green, full gate + migration reversibility verified.

title: Spec 003 — Agent Runtime & AI Gateway
description: SL-A. Provider-agnostic AI gateway (Anthropic/OpenAI/Ollama/vLLM, per-tenant BYO keys); agent definitions bound to one business; run loop with a tool registry (internal tools + MCP host); autonomy-gate IMPLEMENTATION (the seam wired in 001); approvals queue; per-run audit via inputs/outputs/decision. First applied to 002's tickets (AI triage/draft, Mode 1). See docs/ROADMAP.md. Regression contract: deterministic agent runs via mock/recorded LLM provider (golden fixtures); autonomy-gate fail-closed pin (gate runs after RBAC, before tool execution); every agent action audited; run records tenant-isolated; tool-allowlist enforcement. Size: L.

title: Spec 002 — Native Support Desk (inbox + ticketing)
description: Native support desk. Per-business inbound email ingest/parse/thread; native tickets (status, queue, priority, assignment, message thread); attachments; outbound reply via notifications. Introduces THIN first cuts of SL-C (events + activity timeline), SL-D (notifications), SL-E (attachments/object storage). No AI in this slice. See docs/ROADMAP.md. Regression contract: ticket/inbox tenant isolation + RLS matrix; email-thread idempotency (same message-id never double-creates); audit on every mutation; attachment MIME-sniff allowlist; outbox exactly-once delivery. Size: L.
notes:
US1 (receive→threaded ticket) COMPLETE: T017-T034, commits 7c81697..d0a9344. US2 (reply/note/bounce) COMPLETE: T035-T043, through 3a75165. US3 (triage: status/priority/tags/assignee + reopen-on-reply) COMPLETE: T044-T051, commits ecc462b..888492c. Final whole-feature review = SHIPPABLE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS: all 7 reqs + security invariants met; full gate green (make test/lint(0)/contract-test/int-test -p1 all pkgs/sec-test; web vitest 40 + build; real-browser Playwright 26 incl 4 US3). Both red-gates (T045 triage, T046 reopen) green. Key US3 mechanics: Triage service (partial-update, per-field old->new audit via new audit.Entry.OldValue, last_message_at untouched, citext tag case-fold); assignee eligibility via SECURITY DEFINER is_eligible_assignee (mig 0021, membership-or-ancestor, RLS-bypass+self-assert, no-oracle 409) + conditional tickets.assign gate (no-oracle 404); reopen audit in ingest_inbound_message DEFINER (mig 0022, same tx, reversible down==0017); yqi new->open on reply/note.

OPEN FOLLOW-UPS: manyforge-64s (P2, assignable-members endpoint -> richer assignee picker; assignee is manual-uuid stopgap), NEW body-cap P3 (httpx.DecodeJSON no MaxBytesReader, pre-existing API-wide), plus pre-existing bu7/iiq/axq/9li/7c0/zzr. US1 hardening backlog (TokenBucket eviction, skew window, multi-RCPT) still open.
NEXT: US4 (BYO email domain + DKIM, T052+).

title: US2 dashboard UI: hierarchy tree, create sub-business, move, archive/restore, rename, delete
description: Surface the US2 backend (sub-business create/move/archive/restore/rename/delete) in the Angular dashboard. Build a tree view from GET /businesses (parent_id), per-node actions, confirmations. Test-first: vitest unit test for the tree builder + Playwright e2e for the hierarchy flows against the running stack.
close_reason: US2 dashboard UI built (tree/create-sub/move/archive/restore/rename/delete), unit + e2e + prod build green, verified live via Playwright MCP

title: US4 — BYO email domain + DKIM (T052-T061)
description: Phase 6 of Spec 002. Tenant-supplied support address/domain: email_domains (3 modes) with DNS TXT ownership challenge, per-domain DKIM keygen+signing (Ed25519/RSA fallback, encrypted private-key ref), custom inbound addresses bound to a verified domain, outbound identity selection (verified custom else system fallback, FR-013). Extends US1 T024 resolution + US2 T039 send path. Folds in manyforge-9li (custom identity + [#ref] subject). Tests-first per TDD gate (T052-T054 must fail before T055-T061). Tracked granularly via specs/002-support-desk/tasks.md checkboxes.
notes: T057 signing portion + T059 done: migration 0023 get_send_identity DEFINER, notify.Mail.DKIM seam, SendSubscriber.Sealer + Handle custom-identity selection (verified domain → custom From + per-domain Ed25519 DKIM sign; miss/nil-sealer/unseal-fail/bad-key → system fallback, never fails send), SMTPSender.Send signs m.DKIM with precedence over static SystemDKIM, main.go shares the IdentityService sealer. T054 (TestSendDKIMSignsReplyFromVerifiedDomain, TestSendSystemFallbackNotCustomSigned) + TestIdentityOutboundSendIdentitySelection GREEN. Full int-test/contract-test/test/lint/sec-test green.

title: List assignable members endpoint for ticket assignee picker
description: US3 T050 frontend could not build a full assignee picker: no endpoint lists a business's assignable member principals (eligible per FR-011). Frontend currently supports unassign + assign-to-self/manual-uuid. Add GET /businesses/{id}/assignable-members (or similar) returning eligible member principals, then enrich the thread-view assignee control into a real picker. Relates to US3/epic manyforge-n0q.

title: SC-011: bounded mail-loop suppression + automated loop test
description: is_auto_reply is detected and stored on inbound messages but never ACTED upon to bound amplification. Acceptable for US2 (outbound is purely agent-initiated, no auto-send loop), but SC-011 requires a mail loop between two automated systems to be detected+suppressed before exceeding a bounded number of tickets/replies, verified by an automated loop test. Implement the bounded suppression + the loop test (Polish phase). Surfaced in US2 final review.

title: US2 test-hardening: pin the ticket.replied producer↔consumer payload seam
description: The #1 integration seam (Reply enqueues a map; SendSubscriber decodes repliedPayload by json tag) is correct today but UNPINNED: no test drives the REAL enqueued event through the subscriber. reply_integration_test only asserts an outbox row exists; send_integration_test builds its own payload map. A future producer-key rename would silently decode to zero values -> get_send_context no-rows -> message dropped as terminal no-op (warn only), no test failure. Add an end-to-end test (call Reply -> read the actual outbox payload -> SendSubscriber.Handle -> assert the Mail) OR a source-level key-set pin, per the project's 'pin it so CI fails loud' rule. Surfaced in US2 final review.

title: US2: reply/note missing new→open status transition (data-model L438)
description: data-model.md L438 specifies that an internal note and an outbound reply both set a ticket new→open if still 'new' (never close/solve implicitly). Neither ticketing.Service.Reply (T037) nor AddNote (T038) implements this transition + its in-tx audit. Surfaced during US2 Task 4 code review. Decide whether to fold into a US2 follow-up or US3 (triage/status). Add the new→open status update (with audit) to both write paths, in the same tx. NOTE: do NOT bump last_message_at on a note (that would resurface an internal note in the customer-activity sort — omission is correct per review).
close_reason: Implemented in T047: Reply and AddNote advance new→open in-tx with pinned ticket.status_changed audit (old_value plumbing). Covered by TestReplyAndNoteAdvanceNewToOpen.

title: Ticket list assembleTicket N+1 — optimize before SC-010 perf gate
description: T031 read slice: assembleTicket issues 3 extra round-trips per ticket (requester + tags + message_count), so a 100-row ticket-list page = 300+ queries. Correct + business-scoped + no security issue, but will likely blow the SC-010 ticket-list p95 budget under load. Optimize via JOIN/lateral/aggregate (single query assembling tags via array_agg, message_count via subquery/denormalized counter, requester via JOIN) when doing US1 Polish SC-010 perf work. Ref f10d4a8 internal/ticketing/service.go assembleTicket. Also (optional, minor): add a direct RequirePermission httptest asserting 404 body for a member lacking tickets.read.

title: Spec 007 — Coding & Review Agents (opencode)
description: Module internal/agents/coding. Repo connector (GitHub/GitLab) via SL-B; ephemeral sandboxed workspace with NO ambient credentials; opencode invoked via the MCP tool host from 003; output is PRs only (Mode 1, never a silent push) + code reviews; runs gated and audited. See docs/ROADMAP.md. Regression contract: sandbox isolation pin (no credential/network leakage); no-ambient-creds pin; no-silent-push pin; opencode invocation contract; push/PR autonomy-gated; every coding action audited. Size: L.

title: Spec 005 — Lite CRM + Activity Timeline
description: Module internal/crm. Contacts, companies, deals/pipeline; unified activity timeline (SL-C hardened) auto-populated from inbox emails + tickets; AI enrichment/draft follow-ups via 003; optional HubSpot/Salesforce connectors reuse SL-B. See docs/ROADMAP.md. Regression contract: contact dedup/merge correctness; cross-source timeline ordering/attribution; tenant isolation; enrichment behind autonomy gate. Size: M.

title: Spec 004 — External Ticketing Connectors (Jira + Zendesk)
description: SL-B. Envelope-encrypted per-tenant credential vault; OAuth2 + API-key connections; signed inbound webhooks; bidirectional sync between native tickets and Jira/Zendesk (external-id mapping, conflict resolution); connector tools so 003 agents act on external tickets. See docs/ROADMAP.md. Regression contract: vault encryption + no-secret-in-logs pin; webhook signature verification; sync idempotency/conflict resolution; SSRF-safe outbound client; calling an external system is external/irreversible -> autonomy-gated + audited. Size: M-L.

title: Plan: permission-resolution algorithm
description: Effective permissions = union of direct+inherited grants; archived-ancestor handling; no-grant-above-own-level (FR-023); scale within SC-007. FR-010/019/021. See plan-inputs.md.

Spec: specs/001-tenant-foundation/spec.md (31 FRs, 11 SCs). Plan-level decisions to resolve in /speckit-plan: specs/001-tenant-foundation/plan-inputs.md.
close_reason: Tenant Foundation (spec 001) complete: US1-US5 (T001-T078) + polish T079-T085. Full gate green; all children closed.

title: Authenticated JSON API lacks request-body-size cap (httpx.DecodeJSON)
description: Final US3 review (T044-T051) flagged: httpx.DecodeJSON streams the request body with no http.MaxBytesReader, and the /api/v1 protected routes have no global request-size middleware (only ReadHeaderTimeout is set; InboundMaxBytes covers only webhook/SMTP). Pre-existing across US1/US2 reply/note and now US3 triage. Authenticated + permission-gated so practical DoS risk is low, but constitution input-validation/defense-in-depth calls for a per-helper or middleware body cap. Add http.MaxBytesReader in DecodeJSON (or a body-limit middleware on protected routes). NOT a US3 regression. Relates to epic manyforge-n0q.

title: US2 follow-up: outbound body_html dropped at MIME layer (text/plain only)
description: body_html is plumbed end-to-end (DB -> get_send_context -> notify.Mail.BodyHTML) but buildMIME (internal/platform/notify/smtp.go) only emits text/plain + BodyText. Harmless in US2 (composer is text-only, body_html always nil) but a latent gap: an API caller can POST body_html, see it stored/returned, yet it's never delivered. Add a multipart/alternative sender (text + html) in a follow-up. Surfaced in US2 final review.

title: US2 doc accuracy: OpenAPI/Mail comments overstate sending identity + [#ref] subject
description: The /reply OpenAPI summary says 'from the verified custom identity if present' and notify.Mail.Subject comment says it 'carries the [#<reply_token>] tag', but US2 sends from the system identity only (get_send_context joins kind='system') and the subject is plain 'Re: ...' (the [#ref] subject-token fallback is NOT built; threading rides the VERP Reply-To). Tighten the docs/comments or mark deferred. Custom-identity/DKIM = US4; [#ref] subject matching = deferred. Surfaced in US2 final review.

title: US1 test-hardening follow-ups (non-blocking)
description:
Minor test/quality items found during US1 review (all units APPROVED; these strengthen pins/coverage). Batch before final US1 review:
1. replytoken: add deterministic TestVerifyReplyTokenNonCanonicalEncoding (fixed key+uuid, padding-bit flips on BOTH id and sig segments) — current flipped-last-byte case only exercises the canonical-base64 malleability defense ~6.25% of runs (threading_idempotency_test.go:46).
2. SMTP: add multi-RCPT fan-out test (two accepted recipients in one DATA → assert two ingests w/ correct per-recipient EnvelopeRecipient); smtp.go:253 loop currently untested for fan-out.
3. webhook: add a test for the sha256= prefix-strip path in verify() (webhook.go:77) — currently dead/untested; or remove the tolerance (YAGNI, contract is bare hex).
4. webhook: clarifying comment on remoteIP() (X-Forwarded-For intentionally ignored — RemoteAddr only, not trusted for routing/auth).
5. smtp_integration_test port reservation: one-line comment that close-then-rebind is inherently racy (test-only).
Found across f2339ec/0eff959/f37d776.

title: Spec 006 — Feedback / Feature-Request Boards
description: Module internal/feedback. Per business/product board; submission (internal + optional public portal); voting; status workflow; dedupe; convert request -> ticket (links to 002); AI clustering/dedupe via 003; optional GitHub-Issues sync via SL-B. See docs/ROADMAP.md. Regression contract: public-portal oracle boundary (unauth submission must not leak which businesses/products exist); voting integrity (one vote per identity); ticket-link integrity; tenant isolation. Size: S-M.

title: US5 polish T085: verify/close bd planning issues 5zt.1-.7
description: Confirm planning children resolved; close epic 5zt once polish lands.
close_reason: T085 verified: planning issues 5zt.1-.7 already closed.
